claimText: The first-ever grassland bird census in Kaziranga National Park was conducted using the Acoustic Monitoring Technique.
evidenceExcerpt: First-ever grassland bird census was held here using Acoustic Monitoring Technique.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent technological applications in wildlife monitoring.

claimText: Kaziranga National Park is located on the Brahmaputra floodplain at the foothills of Karbi-Anglong.
evidenceExcerpt: Location: Brahmaputra floodplain, at foothills of Karbi-Anglong.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical setting of national parks.

claimText: The Diffalu and Moradifalu rivers flow through Kaziranga National Park.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching rivers to national parks.

claimText: The vegetation of Kaziranga National Park includes alluvial grasslands, semi-evergreen forests, and moist deciduous forests.
evidenceExcerpt: Forest type: Alluvial grasslands, semi-evergreen and moist deciduous forests, wetlands, and sandy chaurs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the vegetation profile of protected areas.

claimText: Kaziranga National Park hosts 70% of the One-Horned Rhino population.
evidenceExcerpt: Key Fauna: One-Horned Rhino (70% of population)
examUse: Statement-based question on species distribution and concentration.

claimText: Kaziranga National Park records the 3rd highest tiger density in India, after Bandipur and Corbett National Parks.
evidenceExcerpt: Recorded 3rd highest tiger density in India after Karnataka's Bandipur and Uttarakhand's Corbett National Park.
examUse: Comparison question on tiger densities across Indian reserves.

claimText: The fauna of Kaziranga National Park includes the Eastern swamp deer, Hoolock gibbon, Capped langur, and Gangetic River Dolphin.
evidenceExcerpt: Key Fauna: ... Eastern swamp deer, Elephant, Hoolock gibbon, Capped langur and Gangetic River Dolphin etc.
examUse: Statement-based question on the presence of specific endangered species in protected areas.

claimText: Kaziranga National Park is recognized as an Important Bird Area by BirdLife International.
evidenceExcerpt: Recognized as an Important Bird Area by BirdLife International.
examUse: Institution-feature question regarding international ecological recognitions.
distractorHooks: Recognized as an Important Bird Area by IUCN
Recognized as an Important Bird Area by WWF
Recognized as a Ramsar Site exclusively for birds
